mnemonics: M.E.T.H.O.D.S - Make Everything Thoroughly Handled, Organized, Defined, and Structured
keyPoints: Methods are blocks of code that perform a specific task and are defined within a class
Methods can take parameters (inputs) and return a value (output)
The void keyword is used when a method does not return any value
commonMistakes: Forgetting to specify a return type (even if it's void)
Not matching the method's parameter list when calling it
Attempting to return a value from a void method or forgetting to return a value from a non-void method
